compiled_state.title: As a plan reviewer, the TDD reviewer references the shared standard and scores instruction-file tasks against the boundary rule
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Update the TDD plan reviewer (tdd.md) to reference the shared behavioral testing standard and add awareness of the instruction-file testing boundary rule. The reviewer should score instruction-file tasks against the boundary rule -- accepting deterministic interface tests (contract schema validation, referential integrity, shim compliance, syntax checks) without demanding behavioral correctness assertions for LLM instruction content.
**Why**: The TDD plan reviewer currently demands behavioral tests for all tasks indiscriminately, causing implementation-plan deadlocks on stories that modify instruction files. With the boundary rule in the shared standard, the reviewer needs to know when structural tests are the correct test type.
**Scope**:
- IN: Add shared standard reference to tdd.md; add scoring guidance for instruction-file tasks against the boundary rule; update exemption criteria awareness to include instruction-file boundary
- OUT: Changes to behavioral-testing-standard.md (sibling story); changes to implementation-plan SKILL.md (sibling story); changes to agent definition files

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, the TDD plan reviewer references the shared standard and scores instruction-file tasks against the boundary rule -- accepting deterministic interface tests without demanding behavioral correctness assertions
  ← Satisfies: "The TDD plan reviewer references the shared standard and scores instruction-file tasks against the boundary rule"
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Testing] Reviewer scoring dimensions may need a new instruction-file-aware criterion or adjustment to existing dimensions
- [Maintainability] The reviewer should reference the standard by path so it auto-consumes future rule changes
